researchNotes: 3 real hits on e-Governance specifically (TN's e-Governance evidence is denser than its Education/Health evidence in the local question bank) plus durable web-sourced facts for Education/Health. The GeM (Government e-Marketplace) question is a good example of a 'mostly true statements plus one wrong year' trap — confirmed correct facts paired with a deliberately wrong launch year. Round-2 pass (2026-07, second expansion): added 'Illam Thedi Kalvi' (2020), TN's COVID-era doorstep remedial education scheme, extending the Education-side coverage which had leaned mostly on Samacheer Kalvi.

reading.intro: Covers Tamil Nadu's education reforms (Samacheer Kalvi), its historic public health leadership, and 3 real exam hits on e-Governance initiatives (E-Sevai centers, the Government e-Marketplace, and the 'Neengal Nalama' scheme).

qa.question: What is Samacheer Kalvi, and when was it introduced?
qa.answer: Samacheer Kalvi ('Uniform System of School Education') was introduced in Tamil Nadu in 2010-11, establishing a standardised curriculum across all school boards (State Board, Matriculation, Anglo-Indian) for classes 1 to 12, aimed at ensuring educational equity regardless of which board a student's school followed.

qa.question: What historic distinction does Tamil Nadu hold in public health legislation?
qa.answer: Tamil Nadu was the FIRST Indian state to enact a Public Health Act, in 1939 — reflecting a long-standing tradition of strong political commitment to public health infrastructure at the district level.

qa.question: Real exam: What is the primary function of TN's E-Sevai centers, per a real exam passage?
qa.answer: To facilitate the online delivery of essential government services — confirmed as the correct inference from a real exam's reading-comprehension-style passage about E-Sevai centers.

qa.question: Real exam: Which statements about the Government e-Marketplace (GeM) are TRUE, and what is the trap?
qa.answer: TRUE: India's public procurement reaches roughly 20-25% of GDP, and GeM was conceptualised as a Special Purpose Vehicle (SPV) for government procurement. The TRAP: GeM was NOT set up in August 2006 — its actual launch was in August 2016; a real exam tested this wrong year as part of a 'choose the correct statements' question.

qa.question: Real exam: What is 'Neengal Nalama', and what does it involve?
qa.answer: A Tamil Nadu Government scheme that is (per a real exam, confirming ALL of the following as true): intended to reduce the gap between people and the Government, an instrument of e-governance, and focused on ensuring government initiatives actually reach their intended beneficiaries.

qa.question: What TN scheme provides subsidized healthcare, and how does it connect to the state's public health history?
qa.answer: The Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS) provides cashless treatment for major illnesses to eligible families — a modern extension of TN's historically strong public health commitment, tracing back to being the first Indian state to enact a Public Health Act in 1939.

qa.question: What is the e-District project, and how does it relate to E-Sevai centers?
qa.answer: A component of the national e-Governance plan implemented in Tamil Nadu, digitizing district-level government service delivery. E-Sevai centers serve as the citizen-facing front-end through which many of these digitized services are actually accessed by the public.

qa.question: Round-2 pass — What is 'Illam Thedi Kalvi' ('Education at Doorstep'), and when was it launched?
qa.answer: A Tamil Nadu Government volunteer-led remedial education scheme launched in 2020, during COVID-19 school closures, sending trained volunteers door-to-door to help schoolchildren (mainly government-school students) keep up with basic learning at home when schools were shut.

questions.prompt: The Government e-Marketplace (GeM) was actually launched in which year (not 2006, a common trap)?
questions.explanation: 2016 (a real exam tested 2006 as a wrong distractor).

questions.prompt: The Chief Minister's Comprehensive Health Insurance Scheme (CMCHIS) primarily provides:
options: Cashless treatment for major illnesses to eligible families
The citizen-facing front-end for accessing digitized government services
questions.explanation: The citizen-facing front-end for digitized government services.

options: Send volunteers door-to-door for remedial learning during COVID-19 school closures
questions.explanation: Volunteer-led doorstep remedial education during pandemic school closures.
